ST_NoA_TadpolePens.IDontRememberKharaa_Question2: Kharaa? I know the Mongolian word but I don't understand what you mean.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.FirstLineText:
Mode: PLACENTA. Phase: VIVARIUM.

This terminal has been reformatted to support PLACENTA/VIVARIUM.

Detecting NoA image on PDA. Boot the Noetic Advisor?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatIsThisPlace: NO INFORMATION AVAILABLE. LOCKED IN MODE PLACENTA/VIVARIUM
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.IsEveryoneGone:
No one’s gone! There were thousands of colonists on the Cicada, stored in quartz memory. They’ll last for billions of years.

But we can’t wake them up until the world is habitable. We need calories to support a large population. We need genes to deal with the heavy metal poisoning and the radiation. We need to know what’s happening to this planet, because something’s wrong with it.

We were trying to find a way…but it all fell apart. Now it’s just you. Everyone is counting on you. All of us. 

And we might be the last people. The last humans, anywhere.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.MaybeShesRight:
No. You can’t give up.

Look at the life outside. The sponges, the corals, the halfmoons—all fighting. Even though they’re dying off. 

Nature gives us ferocity because we must be fierce to live. You must keep fighting!
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.ImGoingNow:
Nothing. I’m just a ghost. 

Stop Nahema. Find the Cicada. Figure out how to survive this planet. 

Give us all a chance to live.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.SoMyJobIsToFixThePlanet:
Or fix us. Humans. So we can live here long term. We can digest the food with adaptations, but the metals, the Masefield...we haven't found a solution. 

Maximum lifespan is about three months.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatIsModePlacentaViviarium:
SUPPORT FOR PAIRED BIOBED AND DIGITIZED HUMAN STORAGE

PATIENT EMULATION FOR ANAMNESIS AND SOCIAL CONNECTION
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.EnoughAboutNahema: She has that effect on people.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.TellMeAboutNahema: She’s our ecologist. She has Masefield. She's going to the tree.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.SoMyJobIsToFindTheCicada_Question: So my job is to find the Cicada?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.SoMyJobIsToFixThePlanet_Question: So my job is to fix the planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhereIsEveryone:
THERE IS 1 OTHER COLONIST IN THIS ROOM

LOCATION: BIOBED STORAGE
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.ItCouldStopReprintingMe: That’s true. That’s true. Or it could roll your memories back, make you start all over again. You can’t let that happen. We don't know how many times it's done that already.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.ImNotSureICantRemember: The NoA must’ve reset your memories. It does that if you get too sick. But I can’t remember it ever taking away our names…
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsMasefield: The call of the sea. It gets in you here. We don’t know how. Something about the proteavirus.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhosChap: The computational dynamicist. He’s my friend. He works for me. He takes care of the beds.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsTheJubileeCreepy: The day when all debts are redeemed and we are free to live in plenty. Freed to live beneath the tree. Freed by tree's new jubilee.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.HowCouldWeBeTheLastPeopleAnywhere: The kharaa. Don’t you remember? How much of you did NoA reset?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsBetweenMeAndTheTree:
The protected area. We didn't go there. Too dangerous.

We made a few expeditions. Ruins. Centuries old-but not many centuries. Not Architect. RECENT ruins.

I don't want to prejudice you. It's very important that you draw your own conclusions, because we messed up. We made very bad mistakes.

Listen. This is important. We are not alone. There is someone else out there. And we made them angry.

I hope they'll treat you as a fresh start. But I can't promise. Try to be decent. And hope they have the same ideas of decency you do.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.PrintTheForkInStorage: UNABLE. UNSHELVING A FORK REQUIRES ANU-NOA AUTHORIZATION.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.PlacentaIDontUnderstand: USER CAN SPEAK TO THE PATIENT STORED IN THE BIOBED
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.DidYouFindAliens:
We all expected to find aliens. Architect ruins. We thought maybe that was why the Cicada diverted here - some secret directive to track down Architect technology. But - it's not that simple. 

This planet is young. Too young for life, Nahema said, especially advanced life. But there's something out there. 

She said this planet was stranger than Earth.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.IWantRescue:
We don't have any Architect ion cubes to power a Neptune. And rescue's not coming. 

You’ve seen our bases, right? When we built those, we found older bases. Bases made by earlier versions of us. Some of them were decades old.

We’re stuck in a loop. We get printed, we try to build a colony, we die, we die so much NoA decides we’re not working out and shelves us. We’ve been trapped here…a long time.

That’s why you can’t give up, understand? If you give up, it’ll all just start again. A new you. But you won’t remember. And sooner or later the coral gardens will be exhausted.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WeWereSupposedToLandOnZezura_Question: We were supposed to land on Zezura. What happened?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsGoingOnWhereAreWe: We’re on Proteus. We crashed. You can’t remember?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatAreYouTryingToStopNahemaFromDoing_Qu: What are you trying to stop Nahema from doing?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatAreYouTryingToStop_Question: What are you trying to stop?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatDoINeedToDo_Question: What do I need to do?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsProteavirus:
What isn't? It's in everything here. Every drop of seawater contains hundreds of millions of proteavirus capsules.

Everything is infected. We don't know if that's why the world is dying.

We thought it was kharaa at first. It has some similarities. Then we thought it might be the remnant of an ancient kharaa infection - some kind of degraded, viral version of the kharaa bacterium.

But it's not that either. 

It's impossible for an alien virus to infect a human. It can't happen. It's like asking a dog to read Greek - the virus doesn't speak human genetics, it can't enter human cells or replicate.

But Nahema thought it might be happening anyway.

We need a virologist.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsBetweenMeAndTheTree_Question: What's between me and the tree?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.YouSaidNotToGoTheTree:
What? Why would I say that? 

Oh. Masefield.

I might have Masefield. You may not be able to trust me.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsGoingOnWhereAreWe_Question: What’s going on? Where are we?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatsTheLastThingYouRemember_Question: What’s the last thing you remember?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhoIsStoredInTheBiobed_Question: Who is stored in the biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhyAreYouInThisBiobed_Question: Why are you in this biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhyDidSheThinkWeCouldntSurviveHere_Quest: Why did she think we couldn’t survive here?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhyDoIHaveToGoToTheTree_Question: Why do I have to go to the tree?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.IThinkMyJobIsToWorkOffMyDebt: Yeah, we all signed the contract. But what does debt mean out here? What’s NoA going to do, repossess your soul? It has us all locked up already.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WeWereSupposedToLandOnZezura:
Yes. But we woke up here. 

Something must’ve gone wrong on the ship. Zyg says we must’ve gone through a phase gate, but there wasn’t a single jump on our flight plan…

It doesn’t matter. We’re here now and we have to make it. We might be the last ones.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.ShesWrong:
Yes. She is. It’s so sad. She’s brilliant...she used to tell me that play was the purpose of life. "What's the point if we can't have fun?" 

But the dark side of that is...when she stops having fun, she doesn't see a point.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhereDoIGoNext: You have to go to the tree.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatDoINeedToDo:
You just can’t give up. 

Yes. I remember! She wanted us to give up. Nahema convinced people we couldn’t survive. She wanted us all to give up and die. 

That’s why I’m here. To tell you not to give up.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.WhatTree: You know. You’ve seen it. I don’t want to talk about it.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.YouSaidNotToGoTheTree_Question: You said not to go to the tree.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.YouSaidNotToGoTheTree_Question2: You said not to go to the tree. You left a quartz chip for new prints warning them not to go to the tree.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens.ImStrandedOnAnAlienPlanetIDontThinkIHave:
You're right. You're here. If you’d rather think of it as an adventure, fine. 

I thought of it as a duty of care, to people in the future. Nahema said…she liked to say this was what humans evolved to do, that this was, in a roundabout way, our idea of fun. 

Well, she used to say that. 

But—whatever you call it—we HAVE to help the people in storage. All of them came here in search of a new life. So many of them are refugees. We can’t just leave them locked up in that quartz tomb forever. 

They went into storage expecting a second chance. Not eternity as a rock.

ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_Investigator_Response: A scientist? Thank God. Dare I hope for a virologist?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_WhyWrongPlanet_Resp:
A ship can’t pass through a phase gate by accident, can it? So either the Cicada was diverted by force, or it diverted itself.

What force, though? Not the colonists. We were all asleep. Not the kharaa—we’d all be dead. The Frontiersmen garrison, maybe?

Or maybe the Cicada detected something it had to chase. Something worth more than a new colony and all the lives aboard.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhatIsAnuNoA_Response:
ADMINISTRATIVE NUCLEUS FOR NOETIC ADVISORS ABOARD AISV CICADA COMMAND MODULE

STATUS UNKNOWN
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhyIsTheForkShelved_Response:
ALL FORKS FROM HABITAT COHORT MARKED BAD AND SHELVED
CAUSE: NEGATIVE MISSION OUTCOMES
SOURCE: HABITAT NOETIC ADVISOR NODE
CHANNEL: HARDLINE CONNECT
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_CampOne_J:
Anita took her people there, to find a way to live in direct contact with the world. It’s a foolish idea. They believe this planet will provide for them. They’ll die over and over until NoA resets them. Or…or perhaps they’re still out there somehow. Who knows what they’ve become. 

Last I knew they'd all vanished. Maybe you're one of them. That would teach me, eh?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_Response_Alt1: As we discussed - Proteus. Certainly not Zezura.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.BootVirtualPatient_Response:
BOOTING FORK IN VIRTUAL EMULATION...     
WHOLE BODY EMULATION...FAILED
WHOLE BRAIN EMULATION...LOADED
INDIVIDUAL SPECIFIC DELTA...MOUNTED
THERAPEUTIC SENSORY LOOP...LOADED
RUNNING TEST BATTERY...DONE
RESUMING BRAIN FUNCTION (SLEEP)...DONE
POLLING ...DONE
AWAKENING...AWAKE
NATURAL SPEECH TO TEXT...ONLINE

Hello. Who's there? I can't get a good look.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Nahema_Wh: Because she’s a jaded burnout ecologist who thinks eels are morally superior to people! And she has lead poisoning, and Masefield. And gout. But we all have gout.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.BootNoAYes_Response:
COPYING NOA IMAGE TO TERMINAL
-----------------------------------
HALT CODE 0xF4

THIS TERMINAL IS IN USE FOR VIRTUAL PATIENT EMULATION.

BOOT VIRTUAL PATIENT EMULATION (VIVARIUM)?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_CanIDoAnythingForYou_Input: Can I do anything for you?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_CanIDoAnythingForYou_Prompt: Can I do anything for you?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_Dont: Did I? Of course I did. Ignore me. It's vital now that you go.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_DoYo: Do I? Yes. Of course. But you must trust me. The stakes are too high.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_WhyWrongPlanet_Like:
Do you ever wonder how we, the human species, learned to fabricate living bodies? I do. I think we stole this technology from the alien ruins discovered on 4546b. Alterra never admitted it, but we Mongolians - we knew.

If we stole immortality from one world of alien relics…what could we steal from another? Certainly it would be worth sacrificing a colony ship to find out. Don’t you think?

Ah, but maybe I'm just a bitter old woman. An old woman in a fabricated young body.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_NoA_Respo:
Do you hate it? Everyone hated it. NoA isn’t evil. It just wants the mission to succeed. It is constrained by rigid rules about human fabrication…what is permitted, what is moral. But it is also compelled to build a successful colony. The conflict is driving it to crisis.

Quaker used to tell me…NoA is insane. But it is usefully insane. “It can confabulate any madness imaginable and many more you cannot imagine, as long as those delusions help it complete the mission.” And Nahema would say, “sounds like any metazoan sensory system.” Ah. She was such a wit.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_WhatIsChanging: Everything. Haven’t you noticed? Use your scanner. Pay attention.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhoIsStored_Response:
FORK OF SOPHIE “BUTCHER” BOUCHER

Gender: W
Age: 96
Mission prospectus role: Xenotrauma surgeon
Current role: Kurultai, triage
Status: unknown

Notes: Assigned nonstandard pay rate "Kurultai" [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Response: Get where? Proteus? Same as you. I accepted resettlement to Zezura and woke up in these charming waters.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_GoodbyeSophie_Response:
Good luck, pioneer.

HALTING PATIENT EMULATION...

DONE.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_TadpoleDepthModule_Response:
Here you are. Mind how you go. This will get you down into the blood of the ocean, but not its heart.

I have to give you one last warning. I made many mistakes. One of them was trusting Nahema. She’s…I cannot defend her. She has become a Linkolist, an ecofascist. She wants to destroy all human presence on this world. Even the sleep bays and everyone inside.

Nahema believes that every living thing in this region depends on that tree for survival. I think she would kill the tree to keep its secrets from us. Starve this part of the world to eradicate the blight...us.

If there is, somewhere out there, a key to human survival on this world—I fear she has gone to destroy it.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WhyExtinct_Inp: How can we be the last people ANYWHERE?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WhyExtinct_Pro: How can we be the last people ANYWHERE?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_HowWrongPlanet_Inpu: How could we be on the wrong planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_HowWrongPlanet_Prom: How could we be on the wrong planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Kharaa_FirstAsk_Response:
How could you forget this? Kharaa is the reason you’re here. You’re a refugee! The kharaa destroyed your home! Our home, our dream!

I’m sorry. 

Back home - in transgov space - we are at war with the kharaa. It starts out as a bacterium. It travels through space on spores, on our ships…and when it infects a place it starts to grow. It creates monsters.

I don’t know if we were losing the war. But we weren’t winning. By now…who knows? It might be on Earth. It might be everywhere.

We thought Proteus was a kharaa world at first. But we were wrong.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_Input: How did you get in this biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_Prompt: How did you get in this biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_Strange_Input: I am an admirer of the strange.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_WhyUnlikely_Re: I am not sure I should tell you.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_WhyUnlikely_Te: I am sure I should not.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_Architect_Input: I am the architect of the future.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_NoJob_Input: I don’t think I have to do a job.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_NoJob_Prompt: I don’t think I have to do a job.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_CampOne_N: I don’t want to talk about that place. We left it behind for good reason.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WhyExtinct_Res:
I told you already. The kharaa. Do you have lead poisoning? 

I'm sorry, that was rude.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_GetRescue_Inpu: I want to go home. There must be a follow-up mission on the way, a signal to send, a ship to build...
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Response: I was…I was…it’s on the tip of my tongue. Quickly, feed me a name.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_HowWrongPlanet_Resp:
I've listened to Ruby and Mantis chew this problem to pulp. It’s their favorite argument.

The Cicada must have made an unscheduled phase gate transit. There are no phase gates at or beyond Zezura...but, nonetheless, we gated.

Ruby believes the destination gate was too close to the surface of this world. The Cicada didn’t have the thrust to escape. So we crashed.

Mantis then asks why anyone would build a phase gate that makes ships crash. A very good question. Is it a trap? If so, where are the trappers?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhatIsAnamnesis_Response: INTERVIEW STORED PATIENT IN VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENT
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_AreThereAliens_What: If we had an observatory I suppose we could look. Twinkle was always fascinated by one object in the sky...I can't remember which.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_AreYouInCharge_Response:
In charge? I guess I would be if anyone would […] listen! 

I’m on the kurultai council. I’m a surgeon, a triage specialist - people look to me to manage crises. I have Chap to keep the biobeds running, Wu to find us more calories, Mantis and Eddie to build more habitat, Zip to salvage whatever she can find, and Nahema to figure out how we can adapt to this hell planet. 

But in the meantime, I must keep us all alive…or at least not permanently dead.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_IsItAw: Is it awful? Not being real?
Is it awful? Not being real?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_Response: It's not "where can I found some airag," I hope.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Kharaa_NotFirstAsk_Response:
It’s a spaceborne bacterium. It used to infect people, but Alterra developed nanotherapies to fight it off. Now it creates these - terrible infestations. It spawns monsters. 

There is reason to believe that the kharaa destroyed a civilization older and vastly more advanced than our own. So the transgovs take it very seriously. They destroyed the Mongolian Independent States just to build a buffer against kharaa.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_DoYouFeelStrange_Alt1_Response: It’s strange to be told ‘maybe I’m you’. So now I feel strange, yes.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_Respon: I’m not in a biobed. I’m talking to you face to face.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_MaybeImYou_Response:
Let me get a look at you.

Damn. My eyes are shot. Do you have a bad knee, photic sneeze, and a compulsion to fix things? Then maybe you are me.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_WhyI:
Look at it! Think, think! Nothing that huge can exist unless it makes its own food! Somehow it acts as a biological reactor - and it feeds everything for hundreds of kilometers. ALL these ecosystems depend on it.

If Nahema destroys that power, she will eliminate any chance of a viable human colony here. The ecosystem will collapse. We will starve. NoA will print you out to die a ghoulish death again and again until it runs out of raw biomass. And who knows? Maybe it will optimize you. Turn you into something wretched. An immortal mind in a glass bottle, tied to a scanner and a solar panel and a propeller. Thrashing around this sea forever.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.FirstLineText:
MODE PLACENTA//VIVARIUM. 

This terminal has been reformatted and paired with a biobed.

Detecting NoA image on PDA. Boot the Noetic Advisor?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Masefield_Response:
My greatest failure. Well. My second greatest, now that Habitat is gone.

I never should’ve let that name catch on. We call it Masefield because of his poem. “I must go down to the sea again, for the call of the running tide - is a wild call and a clear call that may not be denied.” 

It’s like narcosis. An urge to…expose yourself to the world. You become drawn to sites of intense viral activity. You begin to fixate on the tree.

Then you vanish. And NoA prints a fresh new copy of you from the day you boarded the Cicada. And it starts again.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FixPlanet_Resp:
Nahema thought the whole planet was dying. She said we were trapped in a '4546b mindset', looking for a disease to cure when the problem was so much bigger. But it’s hard to know if she could see past her own despair.

If you cannot fix the world, fix yourself. Find adaptations. If you can thrive, anyone can. It has a nice ring to it, doesn’t it?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_GetRescue_Resp:
No one's coming. Even if we could build a Neptune, we don't have Architect ion cubes to power it, or any idea where to find the closest phase gate.

We’ve found bases decades old. Sometimes they were built by earlier versions of us.

We’re stuck in a loop. We get printed, we try to build a colony, we die, we die so much NoA decides we’re not working out and shelves us. It waits for the environment to change, or it assembles a different combination of colonists. It tries again.

We’ve been trapped here…a long time.

If you give up, it’ll all just start again. A new you. But you won’t remember. And maybe next time there won’t be enough resources. Or the coral gardens will be dead.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_SeaCreature_Response: No, you are not. You are a human being. Never forget that.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_CanIDoAnythingForYou_Response:
No. I’m just a ghost. The real Sophie left me here to tell you — don’t give up.

I suppose, if you want, you could come back now and then and tell me how you’re doing, or what become of the real physical Sophie. 

But understand that if you abandon me forever, I will never know: and that is perfectly fine. I will be at peace.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Nahema_Ma: No. You CANNOT give up. Are you less than a fish? Less than a geordie, a halfmoon, a coral polyp? They are all fighting! Look out there, look at them! They are all dying off but they will never give up. You must keep fighting!
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WorkOffDebt_In: NoA says my job is to work off my debt.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WorkOffDebt_Pr: NoA says my job is to work off my debt.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_WhoAmIIDontKnow_Response:
NoA took your name? No. It can’t do that. It can roll you back to an earlier state but it can’t just edit you…can it? I promised everyone it couldn’t do that.

Perhaps…it could cause a declarative memory deficit after printing you, by damaging your temporal lobe or diencephalon…can it do that? Would it allow itself such a crude intervention?

I should’ve made sure. I’m sorry.

What should I call you?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_AreThereAliens_Resp:
Not the ones we expected. We thought maybe that was how the Cicada ended up here - an encounter with advanced alien technology, a secret directive to capture it for Alterra.

But it’s not that simple. There is no sign of an advanced technological civilization here. Not advanced compared to us, at least.

Nahema said this world was young…
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_IKnowKharaa_FirstAsk_Response:
Of course you do. It destroyed our home. Made us refugees.

It starts out as a bacterium. It travels through space on spores, on our ships…and when it infects a place it starts to grow. It creates monsters.

I don’t know if we were losing the war. But we weren’t winning. By now…who knows? It might be on Earth. It might be everywhere.

We thought Proteus was a kharaa world at first. But we were wrong.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Nahema_Re:
Oh, Nahema. What did you do?

We used to be friends, you know. Before she decided…well, there is no use explaining her ideas. They become contagious. Like the virus she loves so much.

Nahema would prefer that humanity go extinct. Here, on Proteus. And perhaps everywhere else as well.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_UltimateColonist_Respons: Oh, good. I've been looking everywhere for you.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_DoYouFeelStrange_NeverMind_Respon: Oh, wonderful. “Do you feel strange? Never mind.” What I would say to a delirious patient who refuses to acknowledge the geordie clamped to her spine.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.ExplainVivarium_Response:
PLACENTA: DEDICATED SUPPORT FOR PAIRED BIOBED/DIGITIZED HUMAN STORAGE

VIVARIUM: PATIENT EMULATION FOR ANAMNESIS INTERVIEW
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.PrintThePerson_Input: Print the person in the biobed
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.PrintThePerson_Prompt: Print the person in the biobed
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_Strange_Response:
So are you therefore a narcissist?

Sorry. My joke - not so good. 

You will have plenty to admire.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FindCicada_Inp: So my job is to find the Cicada?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FindCicada_Pro: So my job is to find the Cicada?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FixPlanet_Inpu: So my job is to fix the planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FixPlanet_Prom: So my job is to fix the planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_Input: So this is Proteus, not Zezura?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_Prompt: So this is Proteus, not Zezura?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_WhyWrongPlanet_Like: Something it had to chase? Like what?
Something it had to chase? Like what?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Nahema_Wh: The kharaa, of course. You must remember the kharaa?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WhyExtinct_Res: The kharaa, of course. You must remember the kharaa?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_Resp: The tree - yes! The tree that isn't a tree, the organism too large to exist. We have to protect it. You must go to the tree! You must stop Nahema from hurting it!
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_Architect_Response:
There is a dreadful double meaning there. Did you intend it?

Well, when we get the colony up and running I’m sure you can be mayor.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_Segments_Response:
They were here before us. They use technology...some of it very strange. Beyond that...anything I could tell you might prejudice you. We never translated their language or found a way to communicate. By the end we were afraid of a full-scale attack.

If they attack you, consider whether you deserved it.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_DoYouFeelStrange_YoureNotReal_Res: This is no time to get philosophical.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.PrintThePerson_Response: UNABLE. FORK SHELVED. UNSHELVING A FORK REQUIRES ANU-NOA AUTHORIZATION
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_WhyWrongPlanet_Inpu: WHY are we on the wrong planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_WhyWrongPlanet_Prom: WHY are we on the wrong planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_AreThereAliens_ButT: We aren’t the ones who established the Protected Zone. We just respect it.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Habitat_R:
We built that place. It was never supposed to be permanent. Just a proving grounds for farming and adaptation. If we could support twenty people, we could scale up to two hundred, then two thousand, a real colony…

But we never learned to support twenty people. The coral gardens ecosystem is too fragile and our farming is too limited. Things got very bad. 

Chap…oh, Chap, what did you do? Nahema told him that the NoA was going to reset everyone. He didn’t want to lose his memory. He blew up the biobeds. There was a fire…the seacocks opened. NoA tried to drown us.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Identity_ImJustMe_Response: We have that in common. Pleased to meet you.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_SoThisIsProteus_Response: We named it Proteus. The site of rapid change. Perhaps someone else has discovered and named it — but that is very unlikely.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_Virtua: Well, say I am — what of it?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_AreThereAliens_What: What about space above this planet?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WorkOffDebt_Ho: What can I do for them?
What can I do for them?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_Input: What do I need to do? What is my purpose?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_Prompt: What do I need to do? What is my purpose?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Input: What's the last thing you remember?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Prompt: What's the last thing you remember?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_WhoAmIIDontKnow_Input: Who am I? I don’t know.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_WhoAmIIDontKnow_Prompt: Who am I? I don’t know.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhoIsStored_Input: Who is stored in this biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.WhoIsStored_Prompt: Who is stored in this biobed?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_WhyI: Why is the tree so important?
Why is the tree so important?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_HowDidYouGetHere_Biobed_IsItAw: Wonderful. A solipsist. Tell me I’m not real when your kidneys start growing marrowbreach teeth and you need them removed. We’ll see who’s a philosopher then!
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_WorkOffDebt_Re:
Yes, we all signed the contract. But what does debt mean out here? There is no money. There are no corporations. The only remnants of Alterra’s power live in your PDA and the NoA.

The only debt we owe is to all the colonists aboard the Cicada, dreaming of new lives.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Nahema_Sh: Yes. She is. It’s so sad. She’s brilliant. She loves life in the abstract. But she can’t feel it, somehow. She loses sight of everything but her resentments. They become as huge and irreducible as the ecosystems she studies. Until the only solution she can see is extinction.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_TadpolePe:
Yes. That’s where we are, isn’t it? I came here to stop Nahema. I remember that clearly. She wasn’t at Habitat during the riot and I thought…this must be her doing. She’s taken a Tadpole, she’s gone to destroy the Tree. I have to stop her…or there’s no hope for anyone. 

My God. Did I abandon my people?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_AreThereAliens_Prot: Yes. The PZ. We'll get to that.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_Response:
You can’t give up.

That’s why I made a copy of myself in this biobed. To tell whoever came next that they MUST NOT give up. 

There were thousands of colonists aboard the Cicada, stored in quartz optical memory. They will last for billions of years. And they are counting on us to awaken them - once we know they can survive.

You must live. You must go to the tree and discover how to thrive. What causes the Masefield call? What's wrong with the ecosystem? Can it be fixed?

Everyone is counting on you. We might be the last people — the last humans anywhere.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Masefield_HowToPrevent_Response:
You can’t.

It's already happening to you. It will keep happening. There is no treatment. You cannot exist on this world without getting Masefield.

And as long as that's true, there is no future for humanity here.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q3_How_Response:
You must reach the tree. You must discover the cause of the Masefield and the viral bloom. Without that - there's no future. Not for us, or for any future version of us. NoA will keep resetting us and trying again until the sea boils.

Find what thrives, and learn its tricks. But find the dead, too. Find out what killed them.

To reach the tree you'll need to go east, into the Protected Zone. It was built by those who came before us...an alien civilization we call the Axum. Their descendants are still here. They call themselves Tailings. Be careful around them.

If you want to go out there, you’ll need a depth module for your Tadpole.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_LastThingYouRemember_Tree_Dont: You told us not to go to the tree.
You told us not to go to the tree.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_FindCicada_Res:
Your job is up to you. There’s no one to give you orders. 

There are 40,000 people on that ship. But even if you found them, you couldn’t print them. There’s no food. There’s metal in the water. There's virus, Masefield...one way or another the planet kills you inside three months. 

It would be pointless immiseration to bring new lives into this world. Unless you can prove it's possible to survive.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_DoYouFeelStrange_YoureNotReal_Inp: You’re not real. You’re a simulation in a biobed.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_DoYouFeelStrange_YoureNotReal_Pro: You’re not real. You’re a simulation in a biobed.
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q2_WhatIsMyPurpose_NoJob_Response:
You’re right. I tried to run Habitat like a mission, with ranks and rotas, and…you’ve seen how that ended.

Nahema used to tell me that life here should be like play. Humans evolved to challenge and survive new environments. She used to say that this should be fun. And if it wasn’t fun, there was no point.

But even if it’s not a job, you have a duty to the people stored aboard the Cicada. So many of them are refugees. We can’t just leave them locked in quartz. Won’t you do that? Save them?
ST_NoA_TadpolePens2.Sophie_Q1_WhereAreWe_HowDoYouKnow_Respon: Zezura has no surface oceans. Nor a gas giant in the sky. Nor a sky that — well, you’ve seen the stars.

ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.WorkGuide_Response:
Приветствую. Я – Зигимантис Тарвидас, бывший инженер фазовых врат. Проработал 60 лет в Степях и выжил благодаря своему правилу: никакой халатности, никакой расточительности. Они выставляют меня в плохом свете. Я этого не люблю.

Вот мой личный рецепт базы. Достаёшь инструмент. Запасаешься титаном, кварцем и медью. 

Строишь отсек. Монтируешь солнечную панель – воздух без электричества не произведёшь.

Ставишь с торцов люки – теперь можно забраться внутрь. Строишь шкафы. Ставишь изготовитель – материалы будут подтягиваться из шкафа автоматически.

Базовый производственный лагерь готов.

[пользователь: «Мантис» Тарвидас]
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Over_Response:
Мы мертвы.

Исследуй этот мир, хотя он и не создан для людей. Скоро ты тоже умрёшь.

Это я тебе гарантирую.

[метаданные повреждены]

ГДЕ-ТО НА ЭТОЙ ПЛАНЕТЕ В КВАРЦЕВОМ БАНКЕ ПАМЯТИ ХРАНЯТСЯ СОРОК ТЫСЯЧ ОЦИФРОВАННЫХ КОЛОНИСТОВ. ОНИ ЖДУТ ВОЗМОЖНОСТИ НАЧАТЬ НОВУЮ ЖИЗНЬ.

ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Over_Response_Line1: This world is yours to explore. But it is not for man.
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Identify_Response:
Привет! Это временное убежище для колонистов, распечатанных в старом Хранилище 3. Так-с, и кто же это у нас?..

ОШИБКА: Не удаётся считать сигнал чёрного ящика. Обратитесь к Чапу Сованну на Базе.

Если ты не помнишь ничего с момента погрузки на «Цикаду», не волнуйся, тебе сделали сброс. Оставайся на месте, мы приплывём и подбросим тебя до Базы.

ДОПОЛНЕНИЕ. ДЕНЬ 670-Й: кабель до Базы перерезан. Придётся тебе добираться самому. Плыви на север. На уговоры плыть куда-то ещё не поддавайся. НИ В КОЕМ СЛУЧАЕ НЕ ПЛЫВИ К ДРЕВУ
[пользователь: «Док» Бушер]
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Alone_Response_Line1: What if you're the last human being in the whole universe?
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Alone_Response:
Что если вы последний человек во вселенной?

Этого нельзя исключать. Что вы думаете по этому поводу?

[метаданные повреждены]

ST_Notifications.PreviewAreaNotification: Разработка этой части игрового мира ещё не завершена. Многие механики, а также строительство отключены. Исследуйте на свой страх и риск.
ST_Notifications.EdgeOfWorldThankyouMessage:
Дальше в тестовой версии продвинуться нельзя.
Спасибо за игру!

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Flora_PlantBulb_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Advise caution. Bioluminescence may signal toxicity, or mark intruders for predator attack.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_1_PDA: Alert! User has consumed raw alien tissue! Full bolus swallow confirmed!
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_3_PDA: Consider that a stationary AI core cannot accompany you into danger, or inject digestive supplements directly into your bowels.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Copper_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Copper deposits may remain in covered areas, like caves or overhangs.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Copper_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Copper is locally scarce. Loose seafloor nodules have likely been raked away by current and storms.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_2_PDA: Crash survivors sometimes develop intense emotional attachments to their PDAs. The introduction of a second computer personality may provoke feelings of guilt or infidelity.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_Scanner_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Mongolian nomads once survived on perfect knowledge of the mare: its temper, milk and blood. Here there are new beasts to know.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_6_PDA: On a scale of one to five, with five being 'instant death', how would you rate your reaction to this food?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_1_PDA: Once rebuilt, the Noetic Advisor system can manage a sensor network to locate the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Flora_PlantBulb_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Recommendation: determine how native organisms respond to light.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_OxygenTankSmall_Pickup_Fir: The Alterra AER tank automatically refills from any oxygen source. The intake filter is preconfigured to reject atmospheric xenon.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Quartz_Pickup_First_1_PDA: The dome-shaped coral colonies embed quartz in their skeletons.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_Scanner_Pickup_First_1_PDA: The scanner tool is a low-field MRI for in situ analysis of anything imaginable. It is illegal, but not always immoral, to generate fabricator blueprints by scanning salvaged technology.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_VehicleBay_Unlock_2_PD: The vehicle blueprints were lost with the Minggatu's licensing server.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_VehicleBay_Unlock_1_PD: This bay constructed and serviced the Minggatu's complement of Tadpole and Starfrog utility vehicles.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Quartz_Pickup_First_2_PDA: This crystal grew around a bioplastic matrix similar to coral. Hypothesis: local coral may form quartz deposits.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Titanium_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Titanium-rich anatase ore. Evidence of a young, highly metallic planet incompatible with known Zezuran geology.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_2_PDA: Xenogenic gout forecaster loaded. Monitoring intestinal weeping for crystal formation.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_3_NoA: "Hope springs eternal in the human breast," as the saying goes. But the human breast will not spring eternal in a xenon-rich atmosphere.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_6_PDA: Assessment: further analysis will impair user survival motive. NoA system, terminate analysis and stand by.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: Be careful out there. Kharaa is endemic in the Ariadne Arm. If this planet is infested, they will certainly investigate your arrival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk4_3_NoA: Both show stress from a major explosion to the aft of the Minggatu. I'm afraid I must downgrade the chances of the ship's survival accordingly.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_2_NoA: But I have the strangest feeling that I understand them. A similar language must be somewhere in my training data, but I can't associate it with any specific context.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_2_NoA: But the Minggatu's loss began with your decision to disregard TSF orders and investigate the alien probe.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_5_NoA: During the descent, the Minggatu jettisoned its starboard EVA shelter. Despite imminent breakup, they were still working to save you -
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_2_NoA: Fortunately, the hazard lockup was not in heavy use on this contract. It landed in soft sand and partially buried itself.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_1_NoA: Hello. I've analyzed your PDA's data on this world. I'm afraid the news is very bad. You will die if you stay here.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_5_NoA: I have added the alien metal to the scanner station's search modes. Be very cautious.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_2_NoA: I must be very clear. Your cultural background may drive you to attempt long term survival. I'm afraid that's simply impossible. Everything here is poison.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_3_NoA: I will make my best guess at a translation. If you find any more, please bring them to me.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_1_NoA: I'm afraid there's no question about it. This column of overgrowth you scanned is the result of eutrophication.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk8_3_NoA: I'm afraid this level of mechanical breakup is a negative sign for the Minggatu's survival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk7_3_NoA: I'm reluctant to speculate without more information. What do you think explains this anomaly?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_3_NoA: I'm sure you recall the last Mongolian contact with alien life had no human survivors.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_1_NoA: I've made my initial analysis of the Minggatu debris field. No reasonable person could blame you for the outcome.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_1_NoA: I've resolved a new contact on the sea surface. It's quite peculiar. It could be the Minggatu, or a fragment of him.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_9_NoA: If so, the Minggatu's keel and command module may have regained control and landed intact - perhaps within a hundred kilometers.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA_scannernot: If you locate any Minggatu debris, search for a sensor node. I could adapt the design into a long-range scanner station.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA_scannerbui: If your scanner station locates any Minggatu debris, please bring me the scan data for analysis.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard hazardous salvage lockup.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard pod maintenance and part stores compartment.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard structural buffer space.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_2_NoA: It could also be the alien probe that pulled you to this world. Whatever the case, I think any expedition should be equipped for a two way journey.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_8_NoA: It is possible the entire starboard work module sheared off the Minggatu at relatively low speed. This could occur during an emergency braking burn.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: It seems to me that our first step should be to locate and scan debris from the Minggatu. That may tell us whether the ship landed intact, and where.
Jailbreaking NoA system. The user must survive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk7_2_NoA: My projected impact point is in error. I don't see how the pod bay ended up on the edge of that cavern.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_2_NoA: Nor do they appear to be Architect relics. This is a possible first contact scenario.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_11_NoA: PDA system, please go to datalink 3. Align with NoA system on user survival motive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_10_NoA: Repairing the Minggatu is your best chance to reach the alien phase gate and find a way home.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_3_NoA: Something is killing this ocean. The most likely cause is global kharaa infection.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk2_2_NoA: Storeroom 3 broke off the Minggatu's starboard module shortly after the emergency shelter was jettisoned. It impacted hard enough to pop the hatches, but survived intact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_4_NoA: The Minggatu followed you through the phase gate, but suffered a propellant tank rupture. It could not establish orbit and suffered uncontrolled re-entry.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_3_NoA: The air is a surgical sedative. The food is loaded with lead, waxes, and heavy metals. The water is radioactive and saturated with virus.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_1_NoA: The artifacts you scanned aren't from the Minggatu. And I hardly need to say that the kharaa don't work with tools.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_2_NoA: The buffer was compressed longitudinally, along the Minggatu's thrust axis, by two major impulses before impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk3_2_NoA: The compartment broke apart on impact. My local node was damaged. I hope you'll do me the favor of taking it permanently offline.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk6_2_NoA: The compartment descended in a stable side-on attitude, slowing its impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_2_NoA: The ecosystem is flooded with nutrients due to mass death somewhere up-current. The result is wild growth...followed by deoxygenation and collapse..
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_1_NoA: The glyphs you found are intriguing, but inconclusive. Elaborate designs don't necessarily imply a semiotic meaning.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_4_NoA: The prior first contact led to the loss of the capital ship Aurora, the extermination of the crew by teleporting cyborgs, and the abduction of an Alterra contractor by an alien intelligence.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk2_3_NoA: The relatively low impact velocity upgrades the probability of the Minggatu's survival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk3_3_NoA: The retrieved data is encouraging. I can project a scenario where damage was limited to the starboard work module, and the rest of the Minggatu landed intact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk1_2_NoA: The starboard EVA shelter was intentionally ejected from the Minggatu. Your crewmates clearly hoped you would survive re-entry.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk4_2_NoA: The starboard EVA workshop and the adjacent propellant tank struck the water as a unit, but split on impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_3_NoA: The starboard pod bay probably broke free in the same event. I would expect to find it very close to these two compartments...
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk6_3_NoA: The triage station controller did register several major injury alerts before losing contact with the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_7_NoA: There is good news. You've located several intact compartments, all from the starboard work module.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: There won't be any need for that. I've retrieved your logs and I'm fully aware of the situation. Crew survival is always my first priority.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_2_NoA: These compartments were added to the Minggatu in a late-life refit. They tore free of their strake at the fabrication attachment points.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk8_2_NoA: This elevator was mounted along a load-bearing structural strake.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_3_NoA: This indicates linear deceleration, not a tumbling breakup. I'm sure that you're as thrilled to hear that as I am.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: Welcome to NoA, the noetic advisor. How can I improve you today?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_3_NoA: When the alien probe began to phase jump home, the Minggatu approached to rescue you, violating quarantine. The TSF warship Andrew Jones fired on the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_3_NoA: When you chose to board the alien probe, were you motivated by the hope of priceless salvage? Or something more altruistic?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk1_3_NoA: While this was very helpful to your survival, it leaves little useful information about the Minggatu's fate.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA: Your life depends on finding and repairing the Minggatu. Searching for debris might give us a clue to its ultimate fate.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_Scan_1_: An electronic computer of alien origin. Its original power supply is missing.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: Assessment: the transformer could accept electrical power from any of your power generator systems.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_NoPower_S: Assessment: these doors are too heavy to open manually. They must be actuated by hydraulics.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ScanStructure_2: Electroforming techniques in the construction indicate the builders generated electrical power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_PowerOn_: Error. Consult NoA unit for data processing.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_Power_Int: Filing work order for cleaning and lubrication.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Sc: Initial assessment: power receiver or generator site. Sediment tracks indicate heavy equipment was dragged out the back.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ApproachingObservatory_Near: It is not the alien probe.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_PowerOn: Jailbreaking alien computer system. The user must survive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ApproachingObservatory_Far_: Magnetic anomaly detected. Possible spacecraft landing site.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ScanStructure_1: Structural age: five hundred to one thousand years. Composition: carved granite blocks coated in silicon bronze and gold.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_ScanStructure_1_: Structural age: two hundred years. Composition: titanium palladium alloy. The sensor masts suggest an early digital civilization.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerOn_1_PDA: The alien transformer is performing nominally. The tower and its attached structure are receiving steady power from our generators.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_Power_Int: The hydraulics that operate these doors are at least seven hundred years old.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Sc: The machinery in this outbuilding has been modified several times over the past five hundred to one thousand years.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Telescope_Scan_: The telescope is not designed for underwater use. Assessment: telescope chamber was originally above the waterline.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_Scan_1_P: This central structure is an orrery depicting the major bodies of an unknown star system. The surrounding wires might represent nearby stars.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: This device once generated power from the flow of hot water. Replicating design for your use.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Terminal_Scan_1: This electronic device is several centuries newer than the surrounding structure.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Graffiti_Scan_1: This engraving was made with a titanium stylus. Age: between fifty and one hundred fifty years.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ReliefMap_Scan_: This is a contour relief map of the surrounding area.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_RTG_Scan_1_PDA: This is a radiothermal generator for extreme long-term power supply. It has been cracked open and the mechanism removed, depriving this sphere of power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Telescope_Scan_: This is a reflecting telescope with a polished silver mirror. It could be steered with an electric motor, but it has no digital computer parts.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Gallery_Scan_1_: This rack could support organisms ranging in size from a small seal to a right whale.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: This structure contains a stepdown transformer to receive high-voltage electricity from a transmission line and pass it to the nearby tower.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectorTether_Scan_1_PDA: This umbilical is flexible and hollow. It contains several insulated power or data cables.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_PowerOn: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Terminal_PowerO: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_PowerOn_: User request. Please gaze directly at the illuminated orrery for a short time.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_Scan_2_P: Without power, it is impossible to determine their implied distance and spectral class.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_5_NoA: An appropriate mythological translation would be 'Proteus'.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_1_NoA: As discoverer, you have the right to assign the indigenous civilization a name from the International Xenoarchaeological Congress rota.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_3_NoA: As soon as I have a result with a useful survival outcome I will alert you.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_4_NoA: But if they designed the probe which brought you here, they may have spacecraft capable of reaching the phase gate in orbit and returning you home.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_5_NoA: I certainly do not want to monopolize your time when you are concerned with critical survival tasks. I'll upload the major findings to your PDA.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_1_NoA: I have made a very important discovery. I hope you'll share my enthusiasm for this breakthrough.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_2_NoA: I will correlate the surrounding stars with astronomical data and determine our location in the galaxy.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_1_NoA: I'm sure you'll agree that the tower you discovered is an alien observatory.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_1_NoA: I'm very eager to meet an alien computer system. I've trained to encounter simulated and real Architect systems, but no NoA unit has ever made first contact with a novel alien computer.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_3_NoA: I've also translated the indigenous name for their world. In most of their cultures, the name means "site of most rapid change".
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_1_NoA: I've retrieved your PDA's images of the alien orrery.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_5_NoA: If the Axum expected to greet visiting Architects, they never came.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_6_NoA: Let me be the first to say: congratulations on your discovery of Proteus, homeworld of the Axum.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_4_NoA: PDA unit, please go to datalink 3.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_5_NoA_AICoreN: Please fabricate an AI core in the same chamber as the alien computer. I've already begun work on a virtual machine based on your scans.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_6_NoA: Please speak to me again if you want to discuss the results. I enjoy conversation on this topic.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_2_NoA: The alien computer system contains a key for translating their language to Architect symbols.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_2_NoA: The alien hardware is built on a systolic architecture and reservoir computing. I've uploaded my precis on the topic to your PDA.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_1_NoA: The designers of the Axum computer system were preparing for contact with living Architects.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_2_NoA: The system contains an Axum-Architect translation dictionary and a greeting message, which I am still deciphering.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_2_NoA: The use of electroforming and stone suggests to me that the builders had not discovered high-temperature metalworking. Perhaps not even fire.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_4_NoA: The word 'change' could also be translated as 'struggle', 'conflict', 'evolution', 'death' or 'play'. Further cultural referents are needed.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_3_NoA: Their understanding of Architect semiosis was badly flawed. I believe the Axum compiled it by observing Architect artifacts or machinery.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_4_NoA: They did not have the advantage of an Architect translation machine, like those present on 4546b and Rakshasa.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_3_NoA: Unless they arrived through a planetside phase gate, like the one used to abduct Robin Ayou from 4546b, the builders must be indigenous to this world.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_4_NoA: We now have a Rosetta stone to translate their language to Architect symbols, and, from there, to human-readable text.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_3_NoA: We're quite familiar with the Architect language from the automatic translation systems they left on 4546b.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_4_NoA: What they know about the Architects might determine the fate of the human species. Crew survival, of course, remains paramount.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_3_NoA: Whoever built this computer lived on a world with an Architect phase gate in orbit. They may have had extended contact with the Architects.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_3_PDA: Be careful - power's low. Don't waste it until you're ready to move.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.ThermalVent_1_PDA: Build a generator on this vent, then bring the Trident close to recharge its power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Power_2_PDA: If you run out of power, you'll be stranded. It might be wise to keep the power off until you're ready to sail.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.EMT_1_PDA: The EMT allows you to operate or tune systems all across the Trident. Keep it handy.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Power_1_PDA: The Trident needs power to move and create air. Power's dangerously low. Find a geothermal vent to recharge.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Scanners_1_PDA: The scanner station will help you locate the geothermal vents and mining sites you need to complete your mission.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Pump_1_PDA: This is a bilge pump. Use your EMT here to pump water in or out of the Trident. Remember, left is lower the water, right is raise!
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Scanners_2_PDA: Use your EMT to align the external scanners on top of the Trident and improve your scanning range.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_2_PDA: Use your repair tool to fix hull breaches, and your EMT to operate the Trident's pumps.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_1_PDA: Welcome to the Void. Your goal is to reach a distant Veps hive and build a sensor pod. But first, you must save your wrecked Trident.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Mine_1_PDA: You will need many more resources to construct the sensor pod. Build a mining platform here to accumulate resources over time.

ST_OxygenGenerator_Carryable.OxygenGeneratorDesc: Медленно производит кислород посредством электролиза. Работает от аккумулятора.

ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfDehydration: Копия погибла. Причина: обезвоживание. Повторная печать.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfHeat: Копия погибла. Причина: тепловой удар. Повторная печать.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfHealth: Копия погибла. Причина: увечье. Повторная печать.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfRadiation: Копия погибла. Причина: облучение. Просчёт контрольной суммы и повторная печать.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfStarvation: Копия погибла. Причина: голод. Повторная печать.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfSuffocation: Копия погибла. Причина: удушье. Повторная печать.

ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_5: Общество, погрязшее под гнётом долгов, не может выжить без надежды на Юбилей – день свободы, когда прощаются долги. Цари древности, восходя на трон, объявляли амнистию. У иудеев каждые 50 лет наступал день искупления.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_6: Но если НоА может обнулить наши воспоминания, как обнулил их Мальку, Юбилей для нас никогда не наступит.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_8: Я должна узнать больше об этом Зове. Нужно что-то делать с этим, иначе мы все кончим, как Малёк.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_4: На четвёртый день биокапсула отпечатывает новую версию Ципоры Мальк. Но она не помнит ничего. Она думает, что мы на Зезуре! Чап говорит, НоА откатил её до «последней рабочей версии», чтобы излечить от отравления – ага, и попутно стереть все лишние мысли. Как, например, Зов, о котором она говорила, что он твердит ей спуститься в пещеру.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_3: Однажды она не вернулась. Проходит два дня, три. Мы все слышим сигнал её чёрного ящика оттуда, со дна пещеры, – значит, она обнулилась. Но НоА её не восстановил. Почему?
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_2: Мы все привыкли, что у нашей главной добытчицы терминальная стадия отравления металлами: дрожь в руках, постоянный озноб. Она всё время проводила в горячих пещерах, а Софи смотрела на это сквозь пальцы. Я приглашала её на наши собрания, но она сказала, что лучше нырнёт ещё раз.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_7: Представьте, что за день до Юбилея царь обнулял бы календарь. Тот, кто не может обрести спасения в работе, станет искать спасения в смерти.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_1: Будущей Аните: Малька больше нет. Не подумай, что это как раньше: её правда нет. Она ушла и не вернулась.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_Whole:
Будущей Аните: Малька больше нет. Не подумай, что это как раньше: её правда нет. Она ушла и не вернулась.

Мы все привыкли, что у нашей главной добытчицы терминальная стадия отравления металлами: дрожь в руках, постоянный озноб. Она всё время проводила в горячих пещерах, а Софи смотрела на это сквозь пальцы. Я приглашала её на наши собрания, но она сказала, что лучше нырнёт ещё раз.

Однажды она не вернулась. Проходит два дня, три. Мы все слышим сигнал её чёрного ящика оттуда, со дна пещеры, – значит, она обнулилась. Но НоА её не восстановил. Почему?

На четвёртый день биокапсула отпечатывает новую версию Ципоры Малёк. Но она не помнит ничего. Она думает, что мы на Зезуре! Чап говорит, НоА откатил её до «последней рабочей версии», чтобы излечить от отравления – ага, и попутно стереть все лишние мысли. Как, например, Зов, о котором она говорила, что он твердит ей спуститься в пещеру.

Общество, погрязшее под гнётом долгов, не может выжить без надежды на Юбилей – день свободы, когда прощаются долги. Цари древности, восходя на трон, объявляли долговую амнистию. У иудеев каждые 50 лет наступал день искупления. 

Но если НоА может обнулить наши воспоминания, как обнулил их Мальку, юбилей для нас никогда не наступит.

Представьте, что за день до Юбилея царь обнулял бы календарь. Тот, кто не может обрести спасения в работе, станет искать спасения в смерти. 

Я должна узнать больше об этом Зове. Нужно что-то делать с этим, иначе мы все кончим, как Малёк.

ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_3: Но вирус вирусу рознь. Вот тут, например: этот коралл нарос на воронке, как какой-то паук. Как... затычка. И он испускает вирофаги – вирусы, убивающие другие вирусы.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_1: Полевой журнал Вандер (не Андера). Я здесь по заданию Наимы, учусь быть биологом. У меня один запасной баллон, точка восстановления – База.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_Whole:
Полевой журнал Вандер (не Андера). Я здесь по заданию Наимы, учусь быть биологом. У меня один запасной баллон, точка восстановления – База.

Итак, у нас у всех протеавирус. Я спросила у Наимы: «Это болезнь? Вроде хараа?» – «Ну ты даёшь, – говорит. – Ты что, не отличаешь бактерию от вируса?» – «Да я ж, – говорю, – ещё только учусь на биолога. По статьям в КПК». 

Но вирус вирусу рознь. Вот тут, например: этот коралл нарос на воронке, как какой-то паук. Как... затычка. И он испускает вирофаги – вирусы, убивающие другие вирусы.

В этом коралле-пауке – один штамм, обычный. И он пытается забороть другой штамм, который лезет из воронки. Наима называет его моровым штаммом.

Зачем вирусу убивать другие вирусы? Надо выяснить.

[пользователь: «Вандер» Кинуын]
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_4: В этом коралле-пауке – один штамм, обычный. И он пытается забороть другой штамм, который лезет из воронки. Наима называет его моровым штаммом.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_2: Итак, у нас у всех протеавирус. Я спросила у Наимы: «Это болезнь? Вроде хараа?» – «Ну ты даёшь, – говорит. – Ты что, не отличаешь бактерию от вируса?» – «Да я ж, – говорю, – ещё только учусь на биолога. По статьям в КПК».
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_5: Зачем вирусу убивать другие вирусы? Надо выяснить.

ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_Whole:
Полевой журнал Андера (не Вандер). Два запасных баллона. Точка восстановления – База. Устал. Интересно, если обнулиться, это поможет от усталости?

Мисс Наима, я осматриваю ту коралловую башню на крабьих ножках. Купольные кораллы внутри обычно пустые, но внутри этого какая-то... белиберда. Как если бы кусок коралла заблудился, испугался и попытался обернуться крабом.

Мы знаем, что протеавирус заражает всё. Мы знаем, что крабы носят эти куполы на головах. Мог вирус частично поменять их местами?

Типа, на Протее соседи через какое-то время становятся родичами.

[вздыхает]

Медузы. Они питаются мёртвой органикой... Где-то там массово мрут живые организмы, и это вызывает неконтролируемое размножение медуз, эвтрофикацию. Рано или поздно они отравят воду, и здесь тоже все умрут.

Вот так и вымирают целые виды.

Я оставлю копию журнала, чтобы пометить границу текущего очага.

ST_POI_CH2_Jubilee1.Audiolog_Whole:
Я переделала своё имя на монгольский лад – Рианнон, дочь Давида, – но до их колоний добраться не успела: эпидемия разразилась, когда я была ещё на Альфе Андромеды.

Поэтому Протей – первая планета, где я живу по-монгольски: в общении с природой, постигая на ходу правила вселенской игры. И я единственный тут картограф.

Я примкнула к Юбилею, потому что Анита права: мы не можем адаптироваться к жизни на Протее, изолировав себя на базах. Мы могли бы жить в океане – добывать воду из слизней, питаться собирательством, спать в воздушных карманах.

Биосфера поможет нам. От «ангелов» мы получили ферменты для переваривания местной пищи – протеавирус каким-то образом помогает их перенести.

При Софи мы только и делали, что боролись с Протеем. А нужно было всего лишь... открыться планете.

Я не могу доказать ей, что мысль уйти внушил им мейсфилд, а в то, что она – марионетка вируса, Анита верить отказывается. Крыса тоже не поверила бы, что паразит гонит её прямиком в лапы кошке.

Анита утверждает, что они смогут выжить прямо в океане. Она убеждена, что планета даст им всё необходимое через «ангелов».

Хочешь сказать, тебя внезапно стали волновать чувства не только планктона, но и окружающих?
